scenarioDrafts.title: Verify Search Functionality and Language-Specific Redirection
scenarioDrafts.text:
Subject: Verify Search Functionality and Language-Specific Redirection
User: no user
Steps:
1. Navigate to page: https://www.wikipedia.org/
2. Type 'Artificial Intelligence' into the search input field (id: 'searchInput').
3. Select 'Deutsch' from the language dropdown (id: 'searchLanguage').
4. Click the 'Search' button.

Expected:
The browser navigates to 'https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spezial:Suche?search=Artificial+Intelligence' (or a similar German search results URL).
The search results page in German is displayed, showing articles related to 'Artificial Intelligence' (e.g., 'Künstliche Intelligenz').
Reason: The primary function of the Wikipedia portal is to enable users to search for information across its vast multilingual encyclopedia. Ensuring that searches correctly redirect to the appropriate language Wikipedia and display relevant results is critical for user satisfaction and data integrity.

Additional:
Category: QUALITY
Discovered page signals:
- Heading: Wikipedia The Free Encyclopedia
- Heading: 1,000,000+ articles
- Heading: 100,000+ articles
- Heading: 10,000+ articles
- Heading: 1,000+ articles
- Heading: 100+ articles
- Element: a #js-link-box-en "English 7,177,000+ articles"

- Element: div [role="search"] "Search Wikipedia EN Afrikaans Shqip العربية Asturianu Azərbaycanca Български 閩南語 / Bân-lâm-gú বাংলা Беларуская Català Če"
- Element: input #searchInput [name="search"]

scenarioDrafts.title: Validate Language Portal Navigation and Article Count Display Consistency
scenarioDrafts.text:
Subject: Validate Language Portal Navigation and Article Count Display Consistency
User: no user
Steps:
1. Navigate to page: https://www.wikipedia.org/
2. Click on the 'Français 2 755 000+ articles' link (href: '//fr.wikipedia.org/').

Expected:
The browser navigates to 'https://fr.wikipedia.org/'.
The French Wikipedia homepage loads successfully.
The page displays information indicating it is the French Wikipedia, and a current article count or similar metric is visible, confirming the scale suggested on the main portal.
Reason: The homepage prominently displays links to various language Wikipedias with their respective article counts. It's crucial that these links accurately direct users to the correct language portal and that the article counts, while not real-time, are generally consistent with the scale of content on the target site, providing a reliable first impression.

Additional:
Category: QUALITY

scenarioDrafts.title: Verify Default Search Language Behavior When No Language is Explicitly Selected
scenarioDrafts.text:
Subject: Verify Default Search Language Behavior When No Language is Explicitly Selected
User: no user
Steps:
1. Navigate to page: https://www.wikipedia.org/
2. Type 'Quantum Physics' into the search input field (id: 'searchInput').
3. Do NOT select any language from the dropdown (id: 'searchLanguage').
4. Click the 'Search' button.

Expected:
The browser navigates to 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Search?search=Quantum+Physics' (or a similar English search results URL).
The search results page in English is displayed, showing articles related to 'Quantum Physics'.
Reason: Users may not always explicitly select a language when searching. The system should have a sensible default behavior, likely defaulting to English given the site's primary language setting, to ensure a smooth user experience and prevent confusion or empty results.

Additional:
Category: QUALITY
Discovered page signals:

discoveryInsight: Semantic discovery: The Wikipedia portal serves as a global gateway to the free online encyclopedia, enabling users to discover and access Wikipedia in various languages, and to search for information across its vast collection of articles. Users: Information seekers/researchers, Users looking for Wikipedia in their native language, Casual browsers exploring knowledge. Goals: Find information on a specific topic, Navigate to Wikipedia in a preferred language, Discover the breadth of content available across different languages.
